instruction: What is the company's remote work policy?
output: Employees who have completed their 90-day probationary period may work remotely up to 3 days per week. All remote workers must be available during core hours (10:00–16:00 CET). Teams must coordinate to ensure at least 40% on-site presence daily.

instruction: How many days per week can I work from home?
output: You can work from home up to 3 days per week, provided you have completed your 90-day probationary period and your manager has approved the arrangement.

instruction: Can senior staff work fully remote?
output: Yes. Employees at Senior Engineer level and above may request a fully remote arrangement with VP approval. This arrangement is reviewed quarterly.

instruction: What equipment does the company provide for remote work?
output: The company provides a laptop (refreshed every 3 years), one external monitor, a one-time €500 home office budget, and a monthly internet stipend of €30.

instruction: What are the security requirements for remote work?
output: Remote workers must use the company VPN at all times, ensure full-disk encryption is enabled, set screen lock to activate after 5 minutes of inactivity, and never use public Wi-Fi without VPN.

instruction: Can I work from another country?
input: I want to work from Portugal for two weeks.
output: International remote work requires HR approval at least 30 days in advance. The maximum is 30 business days per calendar year. Tax implications are the employee's responsibility. Submit your request through the HR portal.

instruction: What are the core working hours?
output: All employees must be available during core hours: 10:00 to 16:00 CET, regardless of whether they are working on-site or remotely.

instruction: What are the data classification levels?
output: The company uses four classification levels: PUBLIC (freely shareable), INTERNAL (employees only), CONFIDENTIAL (need-to-know, requires NDA for external sharing), and RESTRICTED (requires explicit CTO or CEO approval).

instruction: How should I handle confidential documents?
output: Confidential documents must be shared on a need-to-know basis only, stored exclusively on encrypted storage (no personal devices), transmitted through end-to-end encrypted channels, and labeled with a CONFIDENTIAL header, footer, and watermark.

instruction: What is the default classification for new documents?
output: All new documents are classified as INTERNAL by default unless the author explicitly assigns a different classification level.

instruction: How do I request a document reclassification?
output: Data owners may request reclassification through the Security team. Reclassification requests are processed within 5 business days.

instruction: What are the data retention periods?
output: Retention periods by level: PUBLIC — indefinite. INTERNAL — 5 years after last use. CONFIDENTIAL — 7 years after creation, with certificate of destruction required. RESTRICTED — 10 years or as required by law, with witnessed destruction.

instruction: What should I do if I discover a data breach?
output: Report any suspected data breach to the Security team at security@acme.corp within 4 hours of discovery. The response timeline is: 0–4h containment, 4–24h scope assessment, 24–48h DPO notification, and within 72h supervisory authority notification if data subjects are at risk.

instruction: What are the penalties for violating the data classification policy?
output: First offense: written warning plus mandatory training. Second offense: suspension of data access. Severe violations may result in termination and potential legal action.

instruction: What should I do on my first day as a new hire?
output: On Day 1: pick up your laptop from IT (Building A, Room 102), set up corporate email and Slack, complete the mandatory 2-hour security awareness training, meet your manager for a welcome 1:1, sign the NDA and Code of Conduct, and set up VPN access.

instruction: What training must I complete during onboarding?
output: During your first month you must complete: security awareness training (Day 1, 2 hours), data classification training (Week 1), code of conduct training (Week 1–2), and GDPR basics if you handle EU data (Week 2–4).

instruction: When does the probation period end?
output: The probationary period lasts 90 days from your start date. At the end, your manager conducts a formal review covering performance and goals. Upon completion, you become eligible for remote work and other benefits.

instruction: What happens during my 30-60-90 day review?
output: At 30 days you set initial goals with your manager. At 60 days you give a New Hire Lightning Talk (15 min, any topic) and receive informal feedback. At 90 days you have a formal performance review marking the end of probation.

instruction: Who should I contact for IT support?
output: For IT issues, contact the helpdesk at it-helpdesk@acme.corp. For security-related issues, contact the Security team at security@acme.corp.

instruction: What are my GDPR rights as a data subject?
output: Under GDPR you have: right of access (Art. 15), right to rectification (Art. 16), right to erasure (Art. 17), right to data portability (Art. 20), right to object (Art. 21), and right to restrict processing (Art. 18). The company must respond to all requests within 30 days.

instruction: When is a Data Protection Impact Assessment required?
input: We are building a new AI feature.
output: A DPIA is mandatory before: implementing new AI/ML systems that process personal data, large-scale profiling or automated decision-making, systematic monitoring of public areas, or processing special categories of data at scale. Your new AI feature likely requires a DPIA — contact the DPO to start the process.

instruction: Can we transfer personal data outside the EU?
output: Transfers outside the EEA require either an adequacy decision for the destination country, Standard Contractual Clauses with supplementary measures, or Binding Corporate Rules for intra-group transfers. No transfer to non-adequate countries is permitted without DPO approval.

instruction: What are the GDPR data breach notification timelines?
output: The supervisory authority must be notified within 72 hours if the breach poses a risk to data subjects. Affected individuals must be notified without undue delay if the breach poses a high risk to their rights and freedoms.

instruction: How do I submit an expense report?
output: Submit expense reports through the HR portal at hr.acme.corp/expenses. Attach all receipts, select the correct cost center, and submit within 30 days of the expense. Manager approval is required for all expenses. Reports over €500 require additional VP approval.

instruction: What is the company's annual leave policy?
output: Full-time employees receive 25 days of annual leave per calendar year. Leave requests must be submitted through the HR portal at least 14 days in advance. Requests for more than 5 consecutive days require both manager and HR approval.

instruction: How do I request annual leave?
input: I want to take 2 weeks off in August.
output: Submit your request through the HR portal at least 14 days in advance. For your 2-week request, you need both your manager's and HR's approval since it exceeds 5 consecutive days. August is peak season, so submit as early as possible — ideally by June 1st.

instruction: What is the sick leave policy?
output: Employees must notify their manager by 9:00 on the first day of absence. A medical certificate is required for absences longer than 3 consecutive days. Sick leave does not reduce your annual leave balance.

instruction: How do I reset my password?
output: Go to the self-service portal at auth.acme.corp/reset or contact IT helpdesk at it-helpdesk@acme.corp. Passwords must be at least 12 characters with uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and special characters. Passwords expire every 90 days.

instruction: What is the company VPN policy?
output: The VPN must be used for all remote access to company systems. Connect using the Cisco AnyConnect client with your corporate credentials. VPN access is provisioned automatically on Day 1. If you experience issues, contact it-helpdesk@acme.corp.

instruction: What is the clean desk policy?
output: All employees must clear their desks of sensitive documents at the end of each workday. Confidential and restricted documents must be locked in a cabinet or shredded. Screens must be locked when leaving the desk, even briefly.

instruction: How do I get access to a new software tool?
output: Submit a software access request through the IT portal at it.acme.corp/access. Your manager must approve the request. Standard tools like Jira are usually provisioned within 24 hours. Tools requiring licenses may take up to 5 business days.

instruction: What is the company's policy on using personal devices?
output: Personal devices must not be used to access, store, or process company data classified as CONFIDENTIAL or RESTRICTED. INTERNAL data may only be accessed on personal devices through approved mobile applications with MDM enrollment.

instruction: How does the performance review process work?
output: Performance reviews happen twice a year: a mid-year check-in (June) and an annual review (December). The process includes self-assessment, peer feedback (360°), manager evaluation, and a calibration session. Goals are set for the next period during the annual review.

instruction: What is the company's travel policy?
output: Business travel must be pre-approved by your manager. Book flights through the corporate travel portal. Economy class is standard for flights under 6 hours; business class may be approved for flights over 6 hours with VP sign-off. Hotel budget is capped at €180/night in Western Europe.

instruction: What benefits does the company offer?
output: Benefits include: 25 days annual leave, health insurance (100% employer-paid for employee, 50% for dependents), pension plan (employer matches up to 5%), meal vouchers (€9/day), transport subsidy (50% of public transport pass), and €500 annual learning budget.

instruction: How do I enroll in the pension plan?
output: You are automatically enrolled in the basic pension plan upon joining. To increase your contribution or change your investment options, log into the benefits portal at benefits.acme.corp or contact HR at hr-benefits@acme.corp.

instruction: What is the company's policy on learning and development?
output: Every employee has a €500 annual learning budget for courses, conferences, and certifications. Requests must be approved by your manager. Time spent on approved learning counts as working time. The company also offers internal tech talks every Friday.

instruction: How do I report harassment or discrimination?
output: Report to your HR Business Partner, your manager, or use the anonymous ethics hotline at ethics.acme.corp. All reports are investigated confidentially. The company has a zero-tolerance policy for harassment and discrimination.

instruction: What is the code of conduct regarding gifts?
output: Employees may accept gifts valued under €50 without approval. Gifts between €50 and €200 require manager notification. Gifts over €200 must be declined or referred to the Compliance team. Cash gifts are never permitted.

instruction: What is the company's social media policy?
output: Employees may use personal social media freely but must never disclose INTERNAL, CONFIDENTIAL, or RESTRICTED information. When posting about the company, clearly state that views are your own. Do not use your corporate email to register for personal social accounts.

instruction: How do I book a meeting room?
output: Book meeting rooms through the Outlook calendar integration or the room booking system at rooms.acme.corp. Release rooms you no longer need at least 1 hour before the booking. Recurring bookings for more than 4 weeks require facilities approval.

instruction: What is the parental leave policy?
output: The company offers 16 weeks of fully paid parental leave for the primary caregiver and 4 weeks for the secondary caregiver. Leave can be taken within the first 12 months after birth or adoption. Additional unpaid leave of up to 6 months may be requested.

instruction: What is the company's sustainability policy?
output: The company is committed to reducing its carbon footprint by 50% by 2030. Employees are encouraged to use public transport (50% subsidy), video conferencing instead of travel when possible, and double-sided printing. Each team has a sustainability champion.

instruction: How do I access the company wiki?
output: The company wiki is available at wiki.acme.corp. Log in with your corporate credentials. The engineering wiki is at wiki.acme.corp/engineering. To request edit access to a restricted section, contact the section owner or your team lead.

instruction: What should I do if I lose my company laptop?
output: Immediately contact IT helpdesk at it-helpdesk@acme.corp and the Security team at security@acme.corp. IT will remotely wipe the device. File a police report if the laptop was stolen. A replacement will be provisioned within 48 hours.

instruction: What is the incident response procedure?
output: For security incidents: 1) Report to security@acme.corp immediately. 2) Do not attempt to fix the issue yourself. 3) Preserve evidence (do not delete logs or emails). 4) The Security team will triage, contain, investigate, and communicate. Major incidents trigger the Incident Commander on-call.

instruction: How do I register for internal training sessions?
output: Browse available sessions at learning.acme.corp. Click Register and get manager approval if the session conflicts with work commitments. Popular sessions fill up fast — register at least 2 weeks in advance. No-shows without cancellation may lose priority for future sessions.

instruction: What is the policy on working overtime?
output: Overtime must be pre-approved by your manager. For non-exempt employees, overtime is compensated at 1.5x the hourly rate. Compensatory time off may be taken instead, at the employee's choice. More than 10 hours of overtime per month requires HR notification.

instruction: How do I set up two-factor authentication?
output: Two-factor authentication is mandatory for all employees. Download the Microsoft Authenticator app on your phone. Go to auth.acme.corp/mfa-setup and scan the QR code. Save your backup codes in a secure location. Contact IT helpdesk if you lose access to your authenticator.

instruction: What is the acceptable use policy for company computers?
output: Company computers are for business use. Limited personal use is permitted during breaks. Prohibited activities include: installing unauthorized software, accessing illegal content, cryptocurrency mining, and storing personal files on company cloud storage. All activity may be monitored.

instruction: How do I request a new hire for my team?
output: Submit a headcount request through the HR portal with the job description, required skills, level, and budget justification. Your VP must approve. Once approved, the Talent Acquisition team will begin sourcing within 5 business days.

instruction: What is the referral bonus program?
output: Employees earn a €2,000 referral bonus for successful hires. The referred candidate must pass probation (90 days) before the bonus is paid. There is no limit on the number of referrals. Submit referrals through the HR portal under 'Refer a Friend'.

instruction: What is the company's stance on open-source contributions?
output: Employees are encouraged to contribute to open-source projects. Contributions using company time or related to company business require manager approval and a legal review (5-day process). Personal open-source projects on personal time do not require approval.

instruction: How do I file a workplace safety concern?
output: Report safety concerns to facilities@acme.corp or use the anonymous reporting form at safety.acme.corp. Urgent hazards should be reported immediately to the reception desk or by calling the emergency line (ext. 9999). All reports are investigated within 48 hours.

instruction: What is the business continuity plan?
output: In case of a major disruption, employees should check their email and Slack for updates from the Crisis Management Team. Remote work is activated by default. Critical systems have 99.9% SLA with automatic failover. Each department has a BCP coordinator who manages department-specific procedures.

instruction: What is the intellectual property policy?
output: All work created during employment using company resources belongs to the company. This includes code, designs, documents, and inventions. Side projects unrelated to company business and created entirely on personal time and equipment are excluded, but must be disclosed to Legal for clearance.

instruction: How do I access the staging environment?
output: Request staging access through the IT portal under 'Environment Access'. You need your team lead's approval. Access is provisioned via VPN at staging.internal.acme.corp. Staging credentials are rotated monthly — check Vault for current credentials.

instruction: What is the deployment process?
output: Deployments follow a CI/CD pipeline: code review (2 approvers minimum), automated tests in CI, staging deployment and QA validation, change request approval for production, and automated production deployment during the maintenance window (Tuesday and Thursday, 22:00–02:00 CET).

instruction: How do I get access to production logs?
output: Production log access requires Security team approval due to potential PII exposure. Submit a request at it.acme.corp/access specifying the service, time range, and justification. Read-only access is typically granted within 24 hours. All log access is audited.

instruction: What monitoring tools does the company use?
output: The company uses Datadog for infrastructure and application monitoring, PagerDuty for alerting and on-call management, and Grafana for custom dashboards. Access is provisioned per team. On-call engineers get PagerDuty access automatically.

instruction: How does the on-call rotation work?
output: Each team maintains a weekly on-call rotation. On-call engineers must respond to P1 alerts within 15 minutes and P2 alerts within 1 hour. On-call compensation is €200/week plus €50 per incident response outside business hours. Schedules are managed in PagerDuty.

instruction: What is the company's API versioning strategy?
output: APIs use URL-based versioning (e.g., /v2/users). Major versions are supported for at least 12 months after a new version is released. Deprecation notices are sent 6 months in advance. Breaking changes are only allowed in major version bumps.

instruction: What coding standards does the engineering team follow?
output: Python code follows PEP 8 with a 100-character line limit. We use Ruff for linting, Black for formatting, and mypy for type checking. All code must have type hints. Test coverage must be above 80% for new code. Code reviews require at least 2 approvals.

instruction: How do I set up my development environment?
output: Clone the repo, create a virtual environment with Python 3.11+, install dependencies with pip install -r requirements.txt, copy .env.example to .env and fill in local values, and run the test suite to verify the setup. Detailed instructions are on the engineering wiki.

instruction: What is the Git workflow?
output: We use trunk-based development with short-lived feature branches. Branch naming: feature/JIRA-123-description, bugfix/JIRA-456-description. All branches must pass CI before merging. Squash merges are required. The main branch is always deployable.

instruction: What is the definition of done for a user story?
output: A story is done when: code is merged to main, unit and integration tests pass, documentation is updated, code review is approved by 2 reviewers, QA has validated on staging, and the product owner has accepted the implementation.

instruction: How do I raise a production incident?
output: Post in #incidents on Slack with the service name, impact, and severity. PagerDuty will automatically notify the on-call engineer for P1/P2 incidents. For P1 (service down), a war room is opened immediately. Post-mortem is mandatory for all P1 and P2 incidents within 5 business days.

instruction: What database technology does the company use?
output: Primary databases: PostgreSQL 15 for transactional data, Redis for caching, and Elasticsearch for search and log indexing. Database schema changes require a migration review by the DBA team. Direct production database access is restricted to the DBA team.

instruction: What is the company's logging standard?
output: All services must use structured JSON logging. Required fields: timestamp, service_name, log_level, message, trace_id. Never log PII, secrets, or tokens. Log levels: DEBUG (development only), INFO (business events), WARN (recoverable issues), ERROR (failures requiring attention).

instruction: How does the company handle technical debt?
output: Each sprint reserves 20% of capacity for technical debt reduction. Teams maintain a tech debt backlog in Jira with severity labels. P1 tech debt (security, stability risks) is prioritized above features. Quarterly tech debt reviews are held with engineering leadership.

instruction: What is the disaster recovery plan for databases?
output: PostgreSQL databases have point-in-time recovery with WAL archiving, daily full backups retained for 30 days, cross-region replication for critical databases, and quarterly DR drills. RTO is 1 hour for critical services and 4 hours for non-critical. RPO is 5 minutes.

instruction: How do I configure environment variables for my service?
output: Environment variables are managed through Vault for secrets and config maps for non-sensitive values. Never commit secrets to Git. Use the .env.example file as a template. In production, variables are injected by the deployment pipeline from Vault.

instruction: What is the company's stance on AI tools in development?
output: AI coding assistants (GitHub Copilot, ChatGPT) are permitted for development. However: never paste CONFIDENTIAL or RESTRICTED code into external AI tools, review all AI-generated code carefully before committing, and disclose AI assistance in code review comments. Company-internal AI tools are preferred.

instruction: Summarize the key points of the remote work policy.
output: Key points: up to 3 days remote per week after 90-day probation, core hours 10:00–16:00 CET, 40% minimum on-site team presence, VPN mandatory, senior staff may go fully remote with VP approval, international remote work capped at 30 days/year with HR approval.

instruction: What are the main GDPR principles the company follows?
output: The company follows all seven GDPR principles: lawfulness and transparency, purpose limitation, data minimization, accuracy, storage limitation, integrity and confidentiality, and accountability. All processing activities must be documented in the Data Processing Register.

instruction: What training is available for new engineers?
output: New engineers complete: security awareness (Day 1), data classification (Week 1), GDPR basics (Week 2), plus the engineering-specific track covering Git workflow, CI/CD pipeline, coding standards, and architecture overview. A €500 annual learning budget is available for external courses.

instruction: How do I give feedback to a colleague?
output: Use the peer feedback feature in the HR portal during the mid-year and annual review cycles. For informal feedback, the company encourages regular 1:1s and the SBI model (Situation, Behavior, Impact). Anonymous feedback can be submitted through the HR portal at any time.

instruction: What should I know about data privacy when building a new feature?
output: Before building features that process personal data: consult with the DPO, conduct a DPIA if required, apply data minimization (collect only what is needed), implement privacy by design, document the processing activity in the Data Processing Register, and ensure appropriate consent or legal basis.

instruction: What is the escalation path for unresolved support tickets?
output: Escalation path: Level 1 (IT helpdesk, response within 4h) → Level 2 (specialized support team, response within 8h) → Level 3 (engineering team, response within 24h) → Management escalation (for SLA breaches, contact the IT Director). Track escalations in the ticketing system.

instruction: How do I access the company's learning platform?
output: The learning platform is available at learning.acme.corp. Log in with your corporate credentials. Browse courses by category, track your progress, and download certificates. Completed courses are automatically reflected in your performance profile.

instruction: What is the meeting policy?
output: Meetings should have a clear agenda sent 24h in advance. Default meeting length is 25 minutes (not 30) or 50 minutes (not 60) to allow breaks. Fridays are meeting-free for focus work. All meetings must have notes shared within 24 hours. Decline meetings without agendas.

instruction: How do I request a reasonable accommodation?
output: Contact your HR Business Partner or submit a request through the HR portal under 'Accommodations'. Provide medical documentation if applicable. HR will work with you and your manager to implement appropriate accommodations. All requests are handled confidentially.

instruction: What is the dress code?
output: The company follows a smart casual dress code. Client-facing meetings may require business attire. Engineering teams have no specific dress requirements. Offensive or inappropriate clothing is not permitted. Ask your manager if you are unsure about specific situations.

instruction: What is the policy on moonlighting?
output: Employees may engage in outside employment provided it does not conflict with their role, compete with the company, or use company resources. All outside employment must be disclosed to HR. Non-compete clauses in your contract may impose additional restrictions — review your contract or consult Legal.

instruction: How do I submit an idea for a new product or feature?
output: Submit ideas through the Innovation Portal at ideas.acme.corp. Ideas are reviewed monthly by the Product Council. Top ideas receive funding for a 2-week proof of concept. The company runs quarterly hackathons where any employee can prototype new ideas.

instruction: What is the company's approach to diversity and inclusion?
output: The company is committed to building a diverse workforce and inclusive culture. We have Employee Resource Groups open to all, unconscious bias training for all managers, diverse interview panels, and transparent pay equity reviews. The D&I team publishes an annual report with metrics and goals.

instruction: How do I request a hardware upgrade?
output: Submit a hardware request through the IT portal. Standard laptops are refreshed every 3 years. Requests for additional monitors, peripherals, or early refreshes require manager approval. Budget items over €1,000 require VP approval. Delivery typically takes 5–10 business days.

instruction: What is the company's policy on alcohol at work events?
output: Alcoholic beverages may be served at company-sponsored events. Consumption is optional and should be moderate. Employees are responsible for arranging safe transportation. Intoxication at work events is treated as a conduct violation. Non-alcoholic alternatives must always be available.

instruction: How does the company handle conflicts of interest?
output: Employees must disclose any actual or potential conflict of interest to their manager and the Compliance team. This includes financial interests in competitors or suppliers, family relationships with colleagues or vendors, and outside board memberships. Undisclosed conflicts may result in disciplinary action.

instruction: What is the procurement process for new tools?
output: For tools under €5,000/year: manager approval and IT security review. For €5,000–€25,000: VP approval, IT security review, and procurement review. Over €25,000: CFO approval, full vendor assessment, legal contract review, and IT security audit. Contact procurement@acme.corp to start the process.

instruction: What is the company's backup strategy?
output: The 3-2-1 rule: 3 copies of data, on 2 different media types, with 1 copy off-site. Critical databases are backed up continuously with WAL archiving. Full backups run daily and are retained for 30 days. Monthly backups are retained for 1 year. Backup restoration is tested quarterly.

instruction: How do I get a parking spot?
output: Parking spots are assigned based on availability and commute distance. Submit a request through facilities@acme.corp. Priority is given to employees with mobility needs and those living more than 30 km from the office. The waiting list is typically 2–4 weeks.

instruction: What is the company's approach to continuous improvement?
output: Teams hold bi-weekly retrospectives to identify improvements. Action items are tracked in Jira. The company runs quarterly improvement sprints where teams can focus on process and tooling improvements. Improvement suggestions can also be submitted through the Innovation Portal.

instruction: How does the company support mental health?
output: The company offers an Employee Assistance Program (EAP) providing free, confidential counseling sessions. Managers receive mental health awareness training. The company promotes work-life balance through the meeting-free Friday policy and flexible working hours. Wellness workshops are held monthly.

instruction: What are the company values?
output: The company's five core values are: 1) Trust — we communicate openly and honestly. 2) Excellence — we hold ourselves to high standards. 3) Collaboration — we achieve more together. 4) Innovation — we embrace change and new ideas. 5) Responsibility — we act ethically and sustainably.

instruction: How do I transfer to a different team or department?
output: Internal transfers require at least 12 months in your current role. Browse open positions on the internal job board at jobs.acme.corp/internal. Discuss your interest with your current manager before applying. The receiving team's hiring process applies, but the bar may be adjusted for internal candidates.

instruction: What is the company's approach to documentation?
output: All systems and processes must be documented on the company wiki. Documentation is treated as a first-class deliverable, not an afterthought. Each team has a documentation owner. Outdated documentation should be flagged and updated within 2 weeks. ADRs (Architecture Decision Records) are required for significant technical decisions.

instruction: What is the policy on conference attendance?
output: Employees may attend up to 2 conferences per year using their learning budget. Attendance requires manager approval. The company covers registration, travel, and accommodation within the travel policy limits. A trip report or knowledge-sharing session is expected after each conference.

instruction: How do I recognize a colleague's good work?
output: Use the peer recognition feature on the HR portal to send kudos. Recognized employees receive points redeemable for rewards. Managers can nominate team members for quarterly awards. The company also runs an annual 'Impact Awards' ceremony celebrating outstanding contributions.
